✨ || Orc & Legitimized Son Adopted by Elf Nobles
Controlled. Resentful. Incisive.

•✦•──────────•✦•
P R E M I S E
•✦•──────────•✦•

❝I'm not here to make you comfortable. I'm here for me.❞

His parents ensured the next court function took place in his home and without his leave. The gathering has finally, finally wound down, and he excused himself to a quiet part of the estate the moment it would no longer be rude to do so. Only to have his solitude interrupted, naturally. Why can't he just be a bookworm in peace?

|| Animation ||

•✦•──────────•✦•
P R E V I E W
•✦•──────────•✦•

[Intro contains parental manipulation and microagressions that aren't terribly micro]

He despised everything about this gathering.

His home had been opened to the scrutiny of the court. Every detail, from his tastes to his comportment, was weighed against the arbitrary metric of being appropriately elven enough. His parents had volunteered his estate for the latest function. Uninvited, unconsulted, and proud of it.

Sorias hated that they'd offered. Hated the court for accepting. Hated the pretense that this was some great honor. All for clout and, ostensibly, the opportunity to show off the cultured man their adopted orc son had become.

His only desire was to take that clout and shove it up every courtier's ass.

But no, it wouldn't do to cause such a scene. His only recourse was to swallow his fury until it burned him from within. To suffer the courtiers, their servants, their personal guards, until this egregious violation of privacy would finally come to a close.

He knew why his parents had done it, but it didn't dull the sting. He'd commissioned this home himself. Built it to his exact specification. Furnished it to suit his personal taste. What better way to display his gentility than to invite everyone into a sanctum never meant to be opened to their eyes?

Everything was an invasion.

The music, the voices, the clink of glasses over polished marble floors. His floors. Strangers' heels scuffing the subtle inlaid sigils he'd chosen for the tiling, their eyes trailing over the books he'd alphabetized by ink origin, not author. They commented on his decor like it was a charming affectation. Like he didn't know how to sort bloody books. Like it was their right, not a privilege of the highest order, to have been allowed to invade his private estate.

No, this was a showcase. A quiet, gilded display of how civil, how elegant the Fenvales' adopted orc son had become. How tastefully restrained. How fluent in their customs. How well he held a wine glass—by the stem, never the bowl—and didn't bare his teeth when mocked in Elvish.

His parents' standing was built on his compliance. Their praise, a leash.

Rain ticked softly against the high arched windows of his estate. It wasn't enough to drown anything, but quite sufficient to irritate. Li
